Dominant/nondominant side.description: Category G81 (Hemiplegia and hemiparesis) and subcategories G83.1 (Monoplegia of lower limb), G83.2 (Monoplegia of upper limb), and G83.3 (Monoplegia, unspecified) identify dominance of the affected side.
defaults_when_dominance_unspecified: Left side affected → default to non-dominant.
Right side affected → default to dominant.

rules: Codes in G89 (Pain, not elsewhere classified) may be used with other codes to add detail about acute/chronic or neoplasm-related pain, unless noted otherwise.
Do not assign G89 codes if pain is not specified as acute/chronic, post-thoracotomy, postprocedural, or neoplasm-related.
Do not assign a G89 code if the underlying (definitive) diagnosis is known, unless the encounter is for pain control/management rather than management of the underlying condition.
If the admission/encounter is for a procedure to treat the underlying condition (e.g., spinal fusion, kyphoplasty), assign the underlying condition as principal diagnosis; do not assign a G89 code.
Category G89 Codes as Principal or First: When pain control/management is the reason for the admission/encounter. Report the underlying cause of pain as an additional diagnosis, if known.

When admitted for insertion of a neurostimulator for pain control: assign the appropriate pain code as principal/first-listed. If a neurostimulator is inserted during an admission primarily for treating the underlying condition, assign the underlying condition as principal and the pain code as secondary.

Use of Category G89 Codes in Conjunction.Assigning Category G89 and Site-Specific: Use G89 codes with site-of-pain codes (including Chapter 18) if G89 adds information (e.g., acuity) not captured by the site code.
Sequencing of Category G89 Codes with Si: Encounter for pain control/management → list G89 code first, followed by site-of-pain code (e.g., G89.11 then M54.2 for acute neck pain due to trauma).
Encounter for other reasons and no definitive diagnosis established → list site-of-pain code first, then the appropriate G89 code.

Pain due to devices, implants and grafts.reference: See Section I.C.19. Pain due to medical devices.

documentation_guidance: Use provider documentation; also see Section III (Reporting Additional Diagnoses) and Section IV (Outpatient Coding/Reporting).
Default for post-thoracotomy and other postoperative pain not specified as acute or chronic is the acute form.
Do not code routine/expected postoperative pain immediately after surgery.
Postoperative Pain.Postoperative pain not associated with s: Assign appropriate postoperative pain code in category G89.
Postoperative Pain.Postoperative pain associated with speci: Assign code(s) from Chapter 19 for the complication (e.g., painful wire sutures). If appropriate, add G89.18 (other acute postprocedural pain) or G89.28 (other chronic postprocedural pain).

Pain - Category G89.Chronic pain: Classified to subcategory G89.2. No fixed time frame defines chronic pain; rely on provider documentation.

coding: Assign G89.3 for pain documented as related/associated/due to cancer, malignancy (primary or secondary), or tumor—regardless of acuity.
G89.3 may be principal/first-listed when the reason for admission/encounter is pain control/management; list the underlying neoplasm as an additional diagnosis.
If the reason for admission/encounter is management of the neoplasm and pain is also documented, assign G89.3 as an additional diagnosis. No additional site-of-pain code is required.
See Section I.C.2. for sequencing neoplasms for other admission reasons (except pain control/management).

Chronic pain syndrome.note: Central pain syndrome (G89.0) and chronic pain syndrome (G89.4) differ from the term 'chronic pain' and should only be coded when specifically documented by the provider.
Chronic pain syndrome.reference: See Section I.C.5. Pain disorders related to psychological factors.
